title: Design a custom error-aware backoff that uses the unsaved_error
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.SmartRetryWorker` (queue `:external_api`, max_attempts 20). Override `backoff/1` so it branches on the exact error type captured in `%Oban.Job{unsaved_error: unsaved_error}`. Logic: (1) if `unsaved_error` contains a `%HTTPoison.Error{reason: :timeout}`-like struct, return `attempt * 30` (30s/60s/90s, linear — network issues don't need exponential), (2) if it contains `%{status: status}` where status is in 500..599, return `trunc(:math.pow(2, attempt) * 30)` (exponential — server had a fault), (3) if it contains a reason `:rate_limited`, return `5 * 60` (5 minutes — respect rate limits), (4) otherwise delegate to `Oban.Worker.backoff(job)` for the default exponential. perform/1 takes `%{"endpoint" => ep}` and calls `MyApp.Client.call(ep)`. Include a `@moduledoc` explaining the three error classes and why each has a different retry strategy. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/smart_retry_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Tests the full `unsaved_error` shape + multi-clause backoff + delegation fallback. The docs only sketch this pattern; a correct answer requires filling in all three branches plus the @moduledoc rationale.
research_citation: Oban.Worker HexDocs — 'The unsaved error map can be used by backoff/1 to calculate a custom backoff based on the exact error that failed the job.'
